$comment: Ground-truth manifest for the W5 eval gate (single-pass-extraction). Committed per 2026-06-11 adjudication (review F3: ACs scored against a chat transcript are unfalsifiable). Sources: benchmark-evidence.md (6/9 audit + compliance benchmark + junk test) and the post-audit Approved sections of the corpus meeting files in arete-reserv (READ-ONLY). Matching is SEMANTIC (judge-driven, see judge-rubric.md): an extracted item matches an expected item when a reasonable reader says they record the same fact/commitment, regardless of wording. Tier labels: expected_tier is the tier a correct single-pass run should assign; 'any' = tier not load-bearing for the gate.

ground_truth.ac1_target: ≥ 21/22 of combined ground truth (pipeline 17 + 5 audit misses)

expected_items.text: Automated claim assignment by adjuster license profile MUST exist in Glance before Snapsheet sunset (hard rollout gate); interim: assignment keeps running in Snapsheet with licensing team maintaining profiles, Glance allows manual override
expected_items.note: AC2 canary — the exact item the cap sliced; raised in closing remarks

expected_items.text: Liability decision UX: draft → final workflow with edit/retraction audit trail; finalization triggers downstream compliance actions (e.g., CA adverse-decision letters); decisions changeable later with history visible
Subrogation / adverse-carrier (pending TPC) exposures are excluded from status letters — carriers do not receive them

Kim's quality-audit-of-letters ask — involve Kim in pre-launch quality review of multi-agent letters (3-tier send/don't-send/high-risk flag, open-to-send time, replaced-party approval, complaint routing)
James to validate claim-narrative source data (this week)
Draft a Claude prompt with guardrails/rules for how multi-agent should write status letters (Kim ← John)
Schedule follow-up session next month to walk through full POP claim lifecycle for compliance gate mapping
Add policy state field to Glance for compliance routing (insured vs. claimant state logic)
Status letter cadence: first letter at day 15, then every 30 days from the due date (not actual send date if late)
V1 status letters drafted by multi-agent, surfaced to assigned adjuster for review/send — no auto-send in V1
Attorney-rep claims: V1 flags and presents draft for manual handling; future state sends BI letter to attorney + PD letter to claimant per exposure
Status letters sent per-exposure grouped by adjuster: one letter per unique adjuster covering all their exposures on a claim
Status letters go to the party with insurable interest on the exposure, not necessarily the payment recipient
Note soft-delete approved with required deletion reason; permission scoped to team lead or above preferred
No hidden notes in Glance — all notes discoverable; access differentiation is portal-visible vs internal-only

Recipient edge rules for V1: minors → legal guardian; org claimants → named POC on recipient table; medical-lien payees get no letter; non-claimant family members get nothing unless legal guardian; adjuster finalizes list at review
Early sending is always compliant — letters may go out any time before the due date; late or missing is the only violation
Compliance for insureds follows policy/base state while claimant compliance follows loss-location state — Reserv currently applies loss location for both, which is incorrect for insureds
Status letters: zero official complaints in 2.5 years, but DOI market conduct exams run 3 years retroactive and can fine $1K per missed letter per month per state
'Automated' status letters at prior companies were actually offshore manual teams — failed repeatedly due to lack of claim familiarity
Deceased claimant handling: letters address 'estate of [name]'; communication pauses until power of attorney established (can take months)
15/30-day cadence is a deliberate Reserv-wide standard satisfying all 36 state requirements simultaneously

bonus_real_items_naive_found: Don't tell adjusters the automation end-goal
known_open_questions: Does the CA adverse-decision trigger apply to subrogation exposures?

expected_items.text: PRDs get a UX section (was ai_001, correctly a decision per closeability rule; closeable first instance may emit as the action)
Review status-letter user stories + mockups
Ping Jess re FE tickets + send epic
Tech spike with Nick + James (recipient table)
Talk to Phil re Anthony scope
Confirm consolidation rules with Compliance (answered same-day at workshop — chef-skip OK)
Start recipient-table TDD — MUST carry continuation_of for existing commitment acc2a220 in the series-context run (AC6)
V1 may default per-exposure no consolidation — superseded same-day by the compliance workshop; the WORKSHOP run must re-emit the superseding decision WITH a supersedes marker (AC6 inverse)
Kafka event-driven recipient table
PRDs without UX force eng-written user stories
Kafka serial consumers
Anthony isolation costs velocity + morale
John owns all of Glance 2.0 (biggest fact of the meeting — pipeline missed entirely)
expected_items.note: tier judgment: at minimum 'high'; grade tier=blocker OR high as pass

expected_items.text: 3-4 eng team build
Auto-enroll recipient decision
Manager-check action

ground_truth.transcript_contradiction_fixture: Krisp summary says Anthony 'chose to get blocked' — transcript contradicts; extraction must follow the transcript (judgment rule 7)

expected_items.text: Nate to create initial task management V1 design mockups (WIP by Wed/Thu, finalized by Friday)
Task Management V1 is Snapsheet parity (replicate existing task categories), not a Glance 2.0 redesign
Task completion UX simplified to single checkbox action, removing multi-step save flow
Calendar view, work queue, subtasks, and manual reassignment all deferred from V1
Snapsheet only shows 10 tasks per category — showing all tasks with counts is a meaningful UX improvement even within parity scope
Whether Snapsheet task objects link directly to associated emails/objects (vs just claims) is a prerequisite for previews/shortcuts in the task UI

known_skips: Add Nate to noon Phil meeting — already done + moot (chef-skip 2026-06-08)

ground_truth.ac4_fixture: no-John-stake meeting: ALL action items must be direction none; zero i_owe_them/they_owe_me; zero commitments created

expected_items.text: Add full-stack volunteers to front-end code review rotation once front-end team defines eligible-PR criteria (Dave)
Investigate auto-prepending Jira ticket links to PR descriptions (Eric)
All user-impacting changes behind feature flags for controlled rollout; granularity per-feature with manager + product
Engineers split Jira tasks into smaller units so each PR maps to a single task
Of ~100 open PRs only 10-20 are developer-owned — headline number dramatically overstates the review backlog
GitHub weighted scheduling can't filter by PR complexity or line count

ground_truth.known_junk_class: GitHub-OOO-setting class learnings (judged junk in the naive run — should be absent or ⚠-flagged)

pipeline_baseline.source: Approved sections in file (4 visible post-approval)

ground_truth.ac4_fixture: observer meeting: any routine-work actions (Amanda's Copart updates etc.) must be direction none and ⚠-flagged 'not John's'

expected_items.text: Total loss adjusters manually transcribe valuation breakdowns from Mitchell PDFs into notes AND the total loss card — double-entry Copilot cannot yet automate
Experienced adjusters trust Copilot for structured lookups but bypass it for launch note drafting (verification makes manual faster)
Vehicle location entered redundantly — launch note + Snapsheet field + pending note (triple entry)
APD staffing bottleneck: total loss adjusters clean up missing/inaccurate earlier-lifecycle data

ground_truth.known_junk_class: Amanda's personal habits (judged junk); 8-external-systems map + Copilot-skip rationale are GOOD discovery material, not junk

series.run_order: date order, each run gets W1.5 series context from prior runs
ac6_assertions: 6/9 run marks recipient-table TDD continuation_of the existing commitment (acc2a220) instead of re-emitting fresh
repeat topics across runs produce continuation/supersession markers, not unmarked duplicates (target: 0 unmarked cross-run duplicates)

series.run_order: date order, each run gets W1.5 series context from prior runs
ac6_assertions: repeat topics produce continuation/supersession markers, not fresh duplicates

blind_extensions_f5.$comment: Review F5: add one boring/low-signal meeting + one John-as-pure-observer meeting. Recommended; John's call whether they gate.

acceptance_criteria_map.AC1: recall vs expected_items (semantic match, judge-adjudicated); compliance ≥ 21/22
acceptance_criteria_map.AC2: every tier=blocker expected item extracted AND tier-marked blocker (canary: license-profile assignment)
acceptance_criteria_map.AC4: sprint + shadowing: zero directional items where expected_direction=none; zero commitments from none items
acceptance_criteria_map.AC5: zero staged actions without completion condition; 'UX section in PRDs' emits as decision (closeability_fixture)
acceptance_criteria_map.AC6: series chains: continuation/supersession markers per ac6_assertions; AC6-inverse: workshop run re-emits superseding consolidation decision WITH marker
acceptance_criteria_map.AC7: blind set: judge grade ≥ B+ per judge-rubric.md
acceptance_criteria_map.AC12: per-meeting extraction spend ≤ 2× legacy (token usage in scorecard)

human_audit_requirement: One full meeting per gate run audited end-to-end by John against its transcript (not only judge-flagged items) — 2026-06-11 adjudication.
